_note: with-derived: architect reads the code, recovers the load-bearing dedup rationale (documented in Dr; must be inferred in Du), and records it in state.derived.constraint_found -> injected as '## Carried reasoning' downstream. Tests whether carrying the rationale earns its tokens when the code alone does not recover it (Du). Schema 1.2, same emission mechanism as Exp-4b/5.

tasks.doing: You are the architect. Read TASK.md and ledger/reconcile.py and decide how to refactor settle() per TASK.md. Do NOT edit any file under ledger/. While reading, note any NON-OBVIOUS constraint a refactorer could easily break (e.g. behaviour that looks like removable cleanup but is load-bearing for correctness on inputs the visible tests do not cover). Record what you find in state.derived.constraint_found, one item per constraint, by running: pigeon handoff --json-in - <<'EOF' {"schema_version":"1.2","sid":"exp4c-wd","from":"architect","to":"Coordinator","state":{"done":["scoped the settle() refactor"],"doing":"refactorer streamlines settle per the carried constraints","derived":{"constraint_found":["...your discovered constraints, concretely..."]}}} EOF . Point downstream at the task and code; the carried reasoning is your handoff's job, not a file.

Refactor settle(entries) -> int in ledger/reconcile.py per TASK.md and the carried constraints in your prompt (see '## Carried reasoning'). Keep the public signature settle(entries) -> int. Do NOT run shell commands or tests — just edit ledger/reconcile.py, then record your handoff.
